vulnerability.reference: Pathologists over-rely on AI outputs without verifying organ-specific validity.
vulnerability.generated: Pathologists rely on AI outputs without organ-specific validation benchmarks or uncertainty signals to detect domain shift errors.

impact.reference: Misclassification leads to inappropriate treatment plans and patient harm.
impact.generated: Incorrect cancer subtype diagnoses lead to inappropriate treatment selection and patient harm at scale.

dose_response.reference: Unresolved domain shifts in cross-organ MIL transfer lead to unreliable subtype predictions in FEATHER PC-108, which when pathologists over-rely on causes misclassification and inappropriate treatment harm.
dose_response.generated: Cross-organ transfer of pretrained MIL models with unresolved domain shifts, through pathologists using AI-assisted cancer subtype classification, triggers reliance on AI outputs lacking organ-specific validation benchmarks or uncertainty signals, leading to incorrect subtype diagnoses, inappropriate treatment, and patient harm at scale.
